data.content_ref: inline:The claim posits an abrupt climate shift causing early-season frosts that destroyed cereal yields, but Egypt—which the account excludes—was equally dependent on cereal agriculture (the Nile inundation-based grain harvest) and would have been similarly vulnerable to a regional temperature drop. The account provides no mechanism explaining why Egypt alone survived diminished when the same climatic event would have impacted its agricultural base, thus failing to account for the differential survival that the problem explicitly highlights.
